mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance, which increases the cost of capital for businesses and dampens economic growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently positive (average > 0.5%) when FEDFUNDS remains above 5% for more than 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a weakening labor market and potential economic recession, which erodes consumer confidence and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.7% is low, but any sharp rise is concerning
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high retail sales (RSAFS) combined with elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates an overheating economy, which forces the central bank to maintain or increase interest rates, negatively impacting equity valuations by raising discount rates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth > 0.5% MoM and CPIAUCSL growth > 0.3% MoM for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts (HOUST) during an economic expansion reflect strong consumer and business confidence, indicating healthy investment and future economic activity, which supports corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: During confirmed economic expansions, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1.5 million units.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 2017 base level signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side weakness, which directly impacts corporate profits and leads to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 100 for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.6651 is slightly above 2017 base
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO falls below 100 for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins strong consumer spending and corporate revenues, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 157087.0 is near all-time highs
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are consistently negative (average < -0.2%) when PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth for 6 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) creates pressure for the Federal Reserve to maintain or increase the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), leading to tighter financial conditions and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 3% YoY and UNRATE < 4% and FEDFUNDS > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL is elevated, UNRATE is low
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where CPIAUCSL is above 3% YoY, UNRATE is below 4%, and FEDFUNDS is above 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth (GDPC1) coupled with moderate inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a healthy and sustainable economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 2% annualized and CPIAUCSL growth < 2% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22506.365 is high, CPIAUCSL is elevated
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized and CPIAUCSL growth is below 2% annualized.
